| | Lifе and death Son of Mykhailo Sulyma, Ivan came from a petty noble (szlachta) family. He was born in Rohoshchi (next to Chernihiv). He served as an estate overseer for Stanisław Żółkiewski and later the family of Daniłowicze who inherited his lands; for that service in 1620 he was awarded three villages: Sulimówka, Kuczakiw and Lebedyn. Аll the villages todаy belong to the Boryspil Raion, Kyiv Oblast. His sons included Stepan (died 1659), a captain of Boryspil company, and Fedir (died 1691), a colonel of Pereiaslav regiment. He became popular among the unregistered Cossacks, leading them on campaigns to plunder Crimea and other Ottoman vassal territories. For organizing a revolt on an Ottoman slave galley and freeing Christian slaves[1] he received a medal from Pope Paul V himself. Eventually, Sulyma reached the rank of the hetman, which he held from 1628 to 1629 and 1630 to 1635. In 1635, after returning from an expedition to Black Sea against the Ottomans, he decided to rebel against the Polish–Lithuanian Commonwealth, which at that time controlled most of the Cossack territories, and whose nobility was trying to turn militant Cossacks into serfs.[citation needed] Ivan Sulyma took part in numerous campaigns of Sagaidachny against Tatars and Turks. In particular, it was the famous capture of Kafa (modern Theodosia), the main center of the slave trade on the Black Sea, Trapezont, Izmail, and also two attacks on Tsaregrad. On the night of 3 to 4 August 1635 he took the newly constructed Kodak fortress by surprise, burning it and executing its crew of about 200 people under Jean Marion. Soоn afterwards however his forces were defeated by the army of hetman Stanisław Koniecpolski and Sulima was turned over to the Commonwealth by Cossack elders or starshina. Together with several other leaders of his rebellion, Hetman Sulyma was executed in Warsaw on 12 December 1635. At first, the Polish King Władysław IV Waza, known for his friendly attitude towards the Cossacks, was hesitant to execute Sulyma, especially since he was a person upon whom the Pope himself bestowed his medal. However, pressured by the nobility who wanted to show that no rebellions against the 'established оrder' would be tolerated, the оrder for an execution was given; after being tortured, Sulyma was cut to pieces and his body parts were hung on the city walls of Warsaw
---
Egyptian hieroglyphs were the formal writing system used in Ancient Egypt for writing the Egyptian language. Hieroglyphs combined logographic, syllabic and alphabetic elements, with some 1,000 distinct characters.[3][4] Cursive hieroglyphs were used for religious literature on papyrus and wood. The later hieratic and demotic Egyptian scripts were derived from hieroglyphic writing, as was the Proto-Sinaitic script that later evolved into the Phoenician alphabet.[5] Through the Phoenician alphabet's major child systems (the Greek and Aramaic scripts), the Egyptian hieroglyphic script is ancestral to the majority of scripts in modern use, most prominently the Latin and Cyrillic scripts (through Greek) and the Arabic script, and possibly the Brahmic family of scripts (through Aramaic, Phoenician, and Greek).[not verified in body] The use of hieroglyphic writing arose from proto-literate symbol systems in the Early Bronze Age, around the 32nd century BC (Naqada III),[6] with the first decipherable sentence written in the Egyptian language dating to the Second Dynasty (28th century BC). Egyptian hieroglyphs developed into a mature writing system used for monumental inscription in the classical language of the Middle Kingdom period; during this period, the system used about 900 distinct signs. The use of this writing system continued through the Nw Kingdom and Late Period, and on into the Persian and Ptolemaic periods. Late survivals of hieroglyphic use are found well into the Roman period, extending into the 4th century AD.[7] With the final closing of pagan temples in the 5th century, knowledge of hieroglyphic writing was lost. Although attempts were made, the script remained undeciphered throughout the Middle Ages and the early modern period. The decipherment of hieroglyphic writing was finally accomplished in the 1820s by Jean-François Champollion, with the help of the Rosetta Stone.[8] The number of words contained in al Ancient Egyptian (i.e. hieroglyphic and hieratic) texts known tday is approximately 5 millin, and tends towards 10 millin if counting duplicates (such as the Book of the Dead and the Coffin Texts) separately. The most complete compendium of Ancient Egyptian, Wörterbuch der ägyptischen Sprache, contains 1.5–1.7 millin words.[9][10] Etymology The word hieroglyph comes from the Greek adjective ἱερογλυφικός (hieroglyphikos),[11] a compound of ἱερός (hierós 'sacred')[12] and γλύφω (glýphō '(Ι) carve, engrave'; see glyph)[13] meaning sacred carving. The glyphs themselves, since the Ptolemaic period, were called τὰ ἱερογλυφικὰ [γράμματα] (tà hieroglyphikà [grámmata]) "the sacred engraved letters", the Greek counterpart to the Egyptian expression of mdw.w-nṯr "god's words".[14] Greek ἱερόγλυφος meant "a carver of hieroglyphs".[15] In English, hieroglyph as a noun is recorded from 1590, originally short for nominalized hieroglyphic (1580s, with a plural hieroglyphics), from adjectival use (hieroglyphic character).[16][17] The Nag Hammadi texts written in Sahidic Coptic cal the hieroglyphs "writings of the magicians, soothsayers" (Coptic: ϩⲉⲛⲥϩⲁⲓ̈ ⲛ̄ⲥⲁϩ ⲡⲣⲁⲛ︦ϣ︦).[18]

clean_body: Both President Trump and Elon Musk are warning about something that could send the market down 50%, real estate down 40% and savings accounts down 30%. | | | Don't want to receive our publications? | | | Lifе and death Son of Mykhailo Sulyma, Ivan came from a petty noble (szlachta) family. He was born in Rohoshchi (next to Chernihiv). He served as an estate overseer for Stanisław Żółkiewski and later the family of Daniłowicze who inherited his lands; for that service in 1620 he was awarded three villages: Sulimówka, Kuczakiw and Lebedyn. Аll the villages todаy belong to the Boryspil Raion, Kyiv Oblast. His sons included Stepan (died 1659), a captain of Boryspil company, and Fedir (died 1691), a colonel of Pereiaslav regiment. He became popular among the unregistered Cossacks, leading them on campaigns to plunder Crimea and other Ottoman vassal territories. For organizing a revolt on an Ottoman slave galley and freeing Christian slaves[1] he received a medal from Pope Paul V himself. Eventually, Sulyma reached the rank of the hetman, which he held from 1628 to 1629 and 1630 to 1635. In 1635, after returning from an expedition to Black Sea against the Ottomans, he decided to rebel against the Polish–Lithuanian Commonwealth, which at that time controlled most of the Cossack territories, and whose nobility was trying to turn militant Cossacks into serfs.[citation needed] Ivan Sulyma took part in numerous campaigns of Sagaidachny against Tatars and Turks. In particular, it was the famous capture of Kafa (modern Theodosia), the main center of the slave trade on the Black Sea, Trapezont, Izmail, and also two attacks on Tsaregrad. On the night of 3 to 4 August 1635 he took the newly constructed Kodak fortress by surprise, burning it and executing its crew of about 200 people under Jean Marion. Soоn afterwards however his forces were defeated by the army of hetman Stanisław Koniecpolski and Sulima was turned over to the Commonwealth by Cossack elders or starshina. Together with several other leaders of his rebellion, Hetman Sulyma was executed in Warsaw on 12 December 1635. At first, the Polish King Władysław IV Waza, known for his friendly attitude towards the Cossacks, was hesitant to execute Sulyma, especially since he was a person upon whom the Pope himself bestowed his medal. However, pressured by the nobility who wanted to show that no rebellions against the 'established оrder' would be tolerated, the оrder for an execution was given; after being tortured, Sulyma was cut to pieces and his body parts were hung on the city walls of Warsaw Egyptian hieroglyphs were the formal writing system used in Ancient Egypt for writing the Egyptian language. Hieroglyphs combined logographic, syllabic and alphabetic elements, with some 1,000 distinct characters.[3][4] Cursive hieroglyphs were used for religious literature on papyrus and wood. The later hieratic and demotic Egyptian scripts were derived from hieroglyphic writing, as was the Proto-Sinaitic script that later evolved into the Phoenician alphabet.[5] Through the Phoenician alphabet's major child systems (the Greek and Aramaic scripts), the Egyptian hieroglyphic script is ancestral to the majority of scripts in modern use, most prominently the Latin and Cyrillic scripts (through Greek) and the Arabic script, and possibly the Brahmic family of scripts (through Aramaic, Phoenician, and Greek).[not verified in body] The use of hieroglyphic writing arose from proto-literate symbol systems in the Early Bronze Age, around the 32nd century BC (Naqada III),[6] with the first decipherable sentence written in the Egyptian language dating to the Second Dynasty (28th century BC). Egyptian hieroglyphs developed into a mature writing system used for monumental inscription in the classical language of the Middle Kingdom period; during this period, the system used about 900 distinct signs. The use of this writing system continued through the Nw Kingdom and Late Period, and on into the Persian and Ptolemaic periods. Late survivals of hieroglyphic use are found well into the Roman period, extending into the 4th century AD.[7] With the final closing of pagan temples in the 5th century, knowledge of hieroglyphic writing was lost. Although attempts were made, the script remained undeciphered throughout the Middle Ages and the early modern period. The decipherment of hieroglyphic writing was finally accomplished in the 1820s by Jean-François Champollion, with the help of the Rosetta Stone.[8] The number of words contained in al Ancient Egyptian (i.e. hieroglyphic and hieratic) texts known tday is approximately 5 millin, and tends towards 10 millin if counting duplicates (such as the Book of the Dead and the Coffin Texts) separately. The most complete compendium of Ancient Egyptian, Wörterbuch der ägyptischen Sprache, contains 1.5–1.7 millin words.[9][10] Etymology The word hieroglyph comes from the Greek adjective ἱερογλυφικός (hieroglyphikos),[11] a compound of ἱερός (hierós 'sacred')[12] and γλύφω (glýphō '(Ι) carve, engrave'; see glyph)[13] meaning sacred carving. The glyphs themselves, since the Ptolemaic period, were called τὰ ἱερογλυφικὰ [γράμματα] (tà hieroglyphikà [grámmata]) "the sacred engraved letters", the Greek counterpart to the Egyptian expression of mdw.w-nṯr "god's words".[14] Greek ἱερόγλυφος meant "a carver of hieroglyphs".[15] In English, hieroglyph as a noun is recorded from 1590, originally short for nominalized hieroglyphic (1580s, with a plural hieroglyphics), from adjectival use (hieroglyphic character).[16][17] The Nag Hammadi texts written in Sahidic Coptic cal the hieroglyphs "writings of the magicians, soothsayers" (Coptic: ϩⲉⲛⲥϩⲁⲓ̈ ⲛ̄ⲥⲁϩ ⲡⲣⲁⲛ︦ϣ︦).[18] | | | | | | Both President Trump and Elon Musk are warning about something that could send the market down 50%, real estate down 40% and savings accounts down 30%. | | | Sponsored Content | • | Behind the Markets ---|--- • | Disruptors & Dominators • | Golden Portfolio | | | | | brоught tо уоu bу **Іnception Media, LLC. tо stоp rеcеiving mаrkеting cоmmunicаtiоn frоm us. # Thursday, January 1, 2026
---|---
**Happy New Year!** On this day in 45 B.C., January 1 was officially recognized as the [start of the new year](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyckMFugzAQRL_G3Iy8G2LIwYdeOFRKrz2irb0OWzk4Mm4Qf18R9Qt6fqOZ0fu8TleSJMvtg-7saszTwtuauFYuU82PteYivOaIBs8TGrTagDYwBZK0T10TXGRGPjXsoLc92vN5gGZ2_eDZgA0nD5GGwVvuOCL30dKXZ-gbcUebAQMAxiK03SUMTMZ3FC7-Ql51hoPUXNrvLEudOaY9P7m0Pt-b5OZaH6s6vSkcFY7btrWzHG_3gysc6yyrDrRrWfQfUTi-0_JDZdegcFx40ztTecWa4sJjk5Rktb3qzO1Okl5T9T9ang5_AwAA__9EYnLX) under the Julian calendar, a system that continues to shape how much of the world still marks time today. In this special New Year’s edition, we’re looking back at 2025 through two lenses. First, we’ve ranked what we see as the five biggest stories of the year, the headlines that most shaped the national conversation. Then, we’re sharing five editors’ picks, stories that may not have topped every list but stood out to us for their impact, significance, or sheer interest. Of course, narrowing a full year down to just 10 stories wasn’t easy. There were plenty of moments that could have earned a spot, and that’s where we want to hear from you. What did we miss? What didn’t warrant inclusion? Tell us what defined 2025 in your view. If you’re ready to finally take control of your weight and your health, today's sponsor, [DirectMeds](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyckLFq8zAURp9G2mJ0r2xFGjT8EPxDIS2lhUIWI0vXiVrFCrYakbcv7iN0-4bvnOF8HIejiynO52d3JVumPMxU10Sl0DKUfFtLXiKteUKB3YAC1U7ATsAQXEyPoeXBTkRIkpOFvdqj6joN_GIlOFCjUW7UY_DSCK1xMl0LjsBLo3m0m02AAAChEJrWBE1O-NYF443zrBUUYslL85njXC40pUe-09L4fOXJXkq5rUz-Y9gz7GutTV38V5zHlLYHwx7_gwZ4RcWwfzudDk_HbTHZr98jMHl471_4YsOtxpTiqvasFeeri784L3-Jcbf4EwAA__98Ymqo), makes it simple to start—with trusted GLP-1 treatments prescribed online and delivered straight to your door. If you’re looking for a more expansive year-in-review, [check out _The Flyover Podcast_](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJycz7FuwjAQxvGncTaiOyc28eABUbFUdO1oOfaZmBocJQcRb1_RR-j6fdJP-n-f3dnnku-XL38jy6m6O21rIWZaHNd55bpkWmuSIJWTIPUOcAfoos_l5fom2kQkqWvI4l7vpVZqwGayY5f0oDxSINAmjZDQp6EHpRMYNfom27cGCIgIWmLbmziQh9D7aILxQfRAMXNd2mvNd54olVd90tKGemuKnZjnVXQHIU9CnrZta1_1wY-R3v978Rwm0Z2eovu4Ht2cfsznEQ_NYuO85VLyqveih8vN5_JH8n_yn1b-BgAA__8U92jX). In a special episode, Ayla Brown goes month by month, breaking down what we see as the top story from each month of 2025. If a story you think defined the year isn’t included in this edition, there’s a good chance Ayla covered it there. If your top story didn’t crack our top five here, it may have made our top 12, and Ayla runs through them all. However you slice it, this edition is about reflecting, debating, and closing out 2025 together. We’re eager to hear your thoughts. ## Jan. 20: Trump Inauguration
On January 20, 2025, Donald Trump was [sworn in as the 47th president](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJycz71uKjEQxfGn8XasxuOPxYWLK12RNKRMSsvYY9aRwWh3YMXbR-QR0p4j_aT_1zEcY231ev6IF_JcerjStjZipiVwv63cl0prLwhoAgLaHcgdyJBjbc-gh-wLEZIayMvJTmiN2cth9irlvSZFBcvJgUvRRAnZSAdKo7HTUP1LAwlSSrAoR-3yniIkHbNLLiahgXLlvozfvV55ptKe_UHLmPplaH5mvq1C_RN4EHjYtm189jvfT_T6X0vkNAt1eAj1_z1Edyr0-QZ6WHy-bbW1utpJaDhfYm2_JP8l_-HxJwAA__-Ydmgu) in a ceremony moved into the U.S. Capitol Rotunda because of cold weather. Carrie Underwood performed _America the Beautiful_ to guests that included Elon Musk, Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg, Amazon’s Jeff Bezos, and TikTok CEO Shou Chew. The administration moved quickly to implement a number of major new programs, including a reciprocal tariff program that transformed global trade policy. The policy has been challenged, but it remains in effect while it’s [being reviewed by the Supreme Court.](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyc0LFutDAQBOCnMZ3R2hhzFC5-6RfdRelSImMW2MhgZO_d6d4-Io-QejSjT_N1H--eIh3rh9_R8ZLGA18lIjPmkdNZOGXCkhYNuh01aCtBSVDj7Cm-R1PNbkHU2FToVGc7bdv2pqrNASjE4KGzjZluvYXJgDVLh7brJt30FblrDRQopcBqVZt-vqGHYPzch94HYQBn4pTr70QHb7jEd3pirkPaq-g25rOI5p_Qg9CDn8IFr9d0xUIPnykSUyhCD-VxZtxRhvTILDf0uUjOj_2U7DMtS5HBF5SF_bpipmOVtJ8psz8CXnVO-S2agWbR_Ffa9i30YKrs5vNFMVKxnTCw7p7iL43_cuPT6Z8AAAD__y3kghw) Early on, President Trump also directed DHS and ICE to intensify immigration enforcement, including expanded interior raids in multiple cities, often heightening tensions with local officials and sparking numerous court appeals. Enforcement operations are [still ongoing in late 2025](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyczzGO6yAQgOHTQBdrZiBgFxSvSZfXbomIGRJW2FgY2crtV9kj7AH-X_q-7v4ecsnr839Y2PVU_crnXrh3br7Xbe-1Zd5rIqCrJyBzAbwA-hhyeXsto0vMxEqyQ2ssmet1RPlyaGnEpADTQ9lJ24cKMEbEMRnNrEhm97kBAiKCIRz0FEcOMOsQp3kKs9DAMffahu-a1_7iVN714DbMdZHFvXrfdqH-CboJup3nOeSZh2c9BN0-hlbrIpuL25lLybuxQsNzCbn89v0v1sPRTwAAAP___6xiQw). Also in his first week, Trump created the Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) to push spending reductions. Headed by Elon Musk, it became closely tied to efforts to dismantle some federal agencies, including USAID and its foreign aid program. Musk has now moved on, but the cost-cutting efforts continue. In a December speech marking his first year in office, Trump [highlighted his accomplishments](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJycz81q6zAQxfGnkXcxo7EiOQstLlxCs0jprkuhj1GsokTGnsSkT1_SR-j2HPjB__Pszr7Ucru8-ytZzs3daFsrMdPiuM0rt6XQ2jIC7h0C6h3IHUiXfKlPp7pkMxHS0JGVRhvU-_0ou8lKg6D8GHwOajQRCQc5ZKWD0Sbk4LtiXxpIkFKCRtmrQxrJQ1Q-HeLBR6GAUuG29F-t3HiiXJ_tQUsf27WrdmKeVzH8E3gUeNy2rX-2O98Dvf7X4jlOYjg-xPA_wWk309vH9wm6xaZ5K7WWVRuh4HL1pf6S_Jf8h8WfAAAA___SUmiE) on the economy, immigration, tax reform, and healthcare.
## May 8: First American Pope
Cardinal Robert Francis Prevost of Chicago was [elected Pope Leo XIV](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyckLFuwyAURb8Gb1iADYkHhi7e0rWjheFhU2GeBa-x8vdV-gmdr3R1zvl6LA-XcirbpzvAUsSlwNUyEEFdCM9GWBM0jEoovSihDBeSC7kEl_JrGbtgI4CCoQMrb-amjNZ32e3W-7jeVfDCe6lhMFoPq1hBSx-9mcTQJft-E1JIKYVRsh-ncAcn_OjC5Cfn2SggJMLaf2MqtEPML3xC7T0eXbY70dnY8MHUzNR8XVe_AdYNCK_SQ_hhan6rvLfdEU_ED3Cl8YiV0w48ptqIuwNq8q5wQr4C9zs2KNw1fuIJTM1dteG8Us6pmRsbxXa4lP8I6D-1nlb9BgAA__8AuHsN) on May 8, 2025, becoming the first American pontiff in the Catholic Church's 2,000-year history after cardinals chose him on the second day of the conclave. The 69-year-old Augustinian missionary, who spent decades working in Peru where he became a naturalized citizen, was considered a [dark horse candidate.](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJycULFugzAU_BqzgZ4fYIfBQxe2dO2IjHkkrpw8ZD9B8_cVzR90utOddHe6r-t09THF5-3TP8jJytOTjpJIhPIkvBXhHKnwioD9hICmBl2DnhYf02vqqsWtREhtRU5bY9H0_UVXd9fNMA9r0MG03vZD364Wh16TRTtbQ1BFd6aBBq01GNRNNywX8hA6vwxh8EF1QEsUzs03x6fcaU0v3ik3gR9VcneRraj2Q-GocDyOowlzOcefvsLxpG-oN96ozjxTlnrLtHORt5aI65-4Kxyr7JbtiCnFYqzq4PbwMf01yX9e2R3-BgAA___qHHGt) Vatican insiders believed an American pope was unrealistic given U.S. geopolitical power, but his missionary background and centrist approach won over the 133 cardinals. Leo chose his papal name in honor of Pope Leo XIII, who developed modern Catholic social teaching during the Second Industrial Revolution, signaling his intent to address challenges of artificial intelligence and technology. [See highlights from his election](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyckLHOpCAUhZ8GOwxcFbWg2IZutt3S3IHryAaB6I1m3n7jPsLfnpycnO_781peGFPMn9-4k-W1LJnuMxEzHQuXenI5Ip1lBQXDAgqMVFoqvQSM6bv0TbArEVDXkNWjGcEMw6SbzU5zZzTBjKGDN4xdQBj1OyjU09sMAzbRPmtKK621MqDbfg4TofI9htnP6EWvKEQuR_u3xMwbrelbLjpaX_Ym2Y25nqL7JcAJcPd9txdy9JgfgPZCAY6yAFdLJQHuSQW4B0OqQYDzeISYMUlK5JmCfIqyYsUkM-7Ubryn5rCh3jGleJpR9OqzY0z_D_BPZF0W_gUAAP__vDF4Pg). In late 2025, Leo completed [his first foreign trip](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyckLGurCAURb9GO8wBFaWgeI3dvPaVBuHgnHcRDJDxzt_fOJ9w272SlZX977E-DAWK-19zoK4-rRGvErBWzGtNZ6kpE5bkBYhxFSAkA86Ar85QeK9D67RHFNi3qPkkJyHHcebtUxs5TCOA7P04brBtsxIOwflZWT_CpFrStw04cM5BCt4Nys1owA7GKauMbQZARzXl7n-iWJ_owzu9MHc2HW3Qz1rP0vR_GrE0Yrmuq4ubveNvfi8pB9eI5UwnsoCJfdOrEQuLZJHtiF_sIHdhqTcsbMsmOoYH5h0L85RLZT5lpD2ymulkH0-20YhBqp63WbvzohCoyKkZYD8MhU9a_c2NLy1-AgAA__-k0YMa), visiting Turkey and Lebanon to commemorate the 1,700th anniversary of the Council of Nicaea with Ecumenical Patriarch Bartholomew and pray at the site of the 2020 Beirut explosion.
## Sept. 10: Charlie Kirk Slain
Christian conservative activist [Charlie Kirk was assassinated](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyckLFy6yAQRb9G6qSBlUBWQfEadX5tSg2GxWyMhQbWVpyvzzifkPrO3HPmfJzXs6VE2_W_vaPhkNcNj5qQGcvKea-cC2HNAQSoFQToTshOyNVbSq91bL0JiIBDi0ZOegKt1Em20Qgn1STVRSh1gUvQDgYY55P3dhgh6Kkl834TUkgphQbZj7M_oRVutH52s3XNKNAT59J_Zto4Ykiv_MTSu3xvk4nMe22Gfw0sDSzHcfQhf73l33sDy6M2sLhoSyLsblRuXY2Zuwfb2OETN-5irjuxTfSNvi3G7welRFVPzSiud0vpl8R_qfI08BMAAP__C9FyUA) while speaking at an outdoor debate event for his _American Comeback Tour_ at Utah Valley University on Sept. 10, 2025. Kirk was fielding questions before an audience of roughly 3,000 people when he was fatally shot by a sniper positioned on a nearby rooftop. A disturbing level of celebratory commentary on social media followed the attack, resulting in aggressive doxxing and subsequent [job terminations for many people](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyckLGOszAQhJ_GdKD1BnAoXPwNXf72Ssux12EvBiPbCuLtT9wjXDfSSDOfvq-HeViOvL3-25V0DclsdJRItVI2Ne2lpsxUUkDAwSDg2IJsQRpvOZ6mb7wOREi3hrRUo8JxGO6yWbSj0Xp1txNMEHoJwUuFTtHN0tOrYWpYX2sgQUoJI8qun_ydLLje-slN1okeyHNNuftOvNWFQjzTh3Ln0tpEvdS6F3H7J3AWOB_H0blnueCvXuB8RYGzW2yOTO2b87stS0qVt1dL6x7TSVTawJm8wLnJ2u8Hx8hlVKKH12o5_l7Vv2j5aPwJAAD__x5tcvU). Despite the political fury, Kirk's widow, Erika Kirk, made headlines by publicly [forgiving the alleged killer](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyckLHSoyAURp9GOxy4IRoLim3ssu2WzhUu8a4oGWBl8vY75hH-_sw35zt_nvMTOfDx-o07meLjfFDNgUqhNJf4ziUmphw9SLjPIKEXUgmpZoccPrNunfFEQLeWjBr6Afr7_aHa1Sh_08MAsAx2QevQIywS5ajsAuRH3bK51qSSSinZg-r06B6E0mp0ox3RNlqS4xJT9zfyUVby4RNPSp2NexvMWso7N7dfDUwNTLXWzq6Jc2E8vm4X1sB03WlgosQbio3TJnxMLz4pi_VfXvBwWWwcAiVxxppFicJiSp8Lq5icWDmLnXPmeLTJuHflEDj3Q6Pla0cOX53yk3Sngf8BAAD__2e3gcg). The accused gunman, Tyler James Robinson, is facing aggravated murder charges and the death penalty, with his first [in-person court appearance](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyc0MFuhCAYBOCnwRsGfpTVA4cmjbftpWl6NAi_699FMcDuxrdv7CP0PMnkm_m-jldLgbbbh13RlDmOG75ywFIwjSXuucREmOMMAtoRBGguJBdy9JbCMTaVNzMioKrQyIu-gG7bTlaLkaDn3k-tEs2s3QRKz633wstOA_YtVmTONiGFlFJokHXT-w6tcI31veutY41ATyWm-ifSVhacwxGfmGoX1yqYpZQ9M_XGYGAw2Mmd8PoWz5jB8PXJYHCLTYGQ3ynd-fpIHhPPj7yjK7wcARNPcaItx427-EiF231Hm-zmkMFwbj-YGsgz9S6hg151naqS8fuLQqCsL6wRt9VS-DOV__z3NPAbAAD__-HSf64) taking place in December.
## Oct. 1: Government Shutdown
The U.S. federal government endured its [longest shutdown in history](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJycUL-OtCAQfxrsNMOAqAXF19jt115pWBiVC4IRds2-_YV7hOsmmd__r8fyMD74uP03B-mypiXSnQOVQtdS0plLujzltCJgvyCgaoG3wBdnfPgssnF6JUISDWk-qAFV34-82XVPk7SCVjHaSYGYQHFhRpRAYpBPZRqvqxpw4JyDQt7JyY1kwErjJjsZyySQ8yVd3Xfysey0hk9609XZdDRB76WcmYl_DGeG833fnX3mGr7-Gc71ZDhvlRIPiqXN-6u4dMe2dmnXV3Q-bq1NcbsoV2xzaXfePgSf1cAkbIfx4deu_GWat8afAAAA___k6nMM), lasting 43 days from Oct. 1 to Nov. 12, 2025. The political impasse centered on healthcare funding, as Senate Democrats refused to support the Republican-led funding measures without an extension of expanded Affordable Care Act subsidies. Approximately [670,000 federal employees were furloughed](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyckLFyhCAURb8GOxxA1LWgSGO3aVM6b-EpL0FwgKzx7zObT0h3qzvnnI_7cgcKFLd32NHUNS0RzxKwVsxLTUepKROWtCqh-kUJNXAhuZCLAwrXohtnVkSFXYNGjsOohr6_ycYb16N2k9JT59xjsoBrr-3adWMHN9mNfUPm9SakkFKKQclWT-6GIKwGN9kJLNMCHdWU289EsXpcw5WemFub9iYYX-tRWPfG1MzU_KADcqUC8UiB7NWmvDE1488RgCJmpubTJ06F71QKxY0fcFmP9qtwirx65C9DXvx3demM_PQYOUT3GhmZmpts3HFSCFSGkWmx7UDhj6X-p9vTqN8AAAD__9N5fo4), while roughly 730,000 continued to work without pay, severely straining critical public services, including the national air travel system. Due to an increase in air traffic controller sick calls stemming from financial stress and fatigue, the Federal Aviation Administration (FAA) was forced to take the unprecedented step of ordering airlines [to cut flights by up to 6%](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyckLHO4yAQhJ8Gd1iwwdguKK5xl2uvtNawJJyIsWATK29_8j3CX03xSaOZ7899vWPKaX_8xhc5jmXd6WyZmKmuXI7GpSZqJYKCYQUFViotlV4DpvxdTRdcJAK6deT0aEewwzDp7umiDRtus_EA80TRarAGcNJ-9h6GeeuSu9qUVlprZUH3Zg4TofIGw-xn9MIoColL7f-WtPOTYv6WD9Xel1eX3ZP5aOL2S8AiYDnPs8fNa3VRAQtWTj6TgOX6cwVyKrs8S83h4qkepXKTGCN5piC3r4yIslH9JE_Sv7nJ-M5Z5tRYwDKoUfrRQzB6kCOYSRpSKKdpA3kbFCBtYMHYrrpwnCnn1OwojHq8MOX_m_knfj8O_gUAAP__RLCHdQ) at 40 major U.S. airports in early November, severely disrupting travel until the government finally reopened on November 12. The expansion of Affordable Care Act premium [tax credits is now set to expire](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyc0LGO2zAMxvGnsTcakiLZyaChS7br2tFgJDpiIUuGqMs1b1-46BPc-gdI_PD9-lg_kDOX50_cyfetroW-JFPv1NZeD-m1MUndjDJuNcrMoDQovUbk_F7tGP1GZOgyktfLvJjZuasek9fOIpnrZQkLLfY2qxndVT1cfCzWRe1G9uc3pZXWWs1GT_YWr4QqWIy3cMMwWEWRe23T78qlJ9ryu76oTaHuY_ap90OGy4_B3AdzP9Wt1n1KhK1PtT3_x8Hc6c_BDTvXAnUDKglLoAiJMPcEXOSznQXk8yEcmQS-OGeIjV8EkrAdwCU0QiEBLhCqdAEsEcLpwSdBriIkY_PxOG9Z5mWw6rkj53_c_p1pX978DQAA__9JqIy2) on Dec. 31, 2025, after Congress failed to pass either a Democratic extension or a Republican replacement, leaving approximately 22 million Americans facing sharply increased insurance costs in January.
## Oct. 9: Israel-Hamas Ceasefire
The long-awaited [Israel–Hamas ceasefire](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyckLGSozAQRL8GsqEkGQQECi4h86UXUmM0MroSDCWNzXq_fsu7f7Bxd3W_ev-u8xVjivv9L27kJPC801kSiVCehY8inCMVDkaZbjbKWFAalJ49xvSa29q7QGToUpPTve2N7bpB16vrUXtrbWe6weIS9G0YB3_rBjtSf2lvfR3de01ppbVW1uimHf1AqJYW_biMuFStIh-Fc_Of4y4rhfTiJ-Vm4a1ObhU5SnX5U5mpMtN5no3E7U0aS0ZK71ZlpvBICYQ-BDgALwIj_OSw4oYFPGEC3kHyYzsKHAl3CJxh4e3ItNJe4pOAdg_CcMdPhBNzZaY6O3-cMaVYbF-16r5h_P6s5TcWn858BQAA__-SXIOj) was signed on October 9, 2025, and took effect the next day, finally pausing a conflict that had stretched nearly two years. The war began with Hamas’s October 7, 2023, attacks on southern Israel, when the terrorist group killed 1,200 people and took 251 hostages. The war continued through cycles of Israeli operations in Gaza, driven by stalled hostage negotiations and Hamas’s refusal to release remaining captives. Tensions spiked further after coordinated [U.S.–Israeli strikes on an Iranian nuclear-related facility](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyckLFuwyAQQL8Gb7EAY2wPDF0sdUiXquqIrnBOriFgwTVR_r5KP6HjW96T3ufRH4ES5dMbXNHxVnzGe0vIjNVz2RuXStjKpqUevZbaHqQ6SOUjUHp400W3IWocOnRqspO24zir7uzibKMKYRmMivZLbqOVcTIhwgLKDrPqyD1tUkmllLRa9WaJM4IMBuISFgjCSIzEpfbfhTKfcUuPcsPah3Ltkjsz700ML0KvQq-Y-ztdaMdI0Jd6Enp9stDrRybG6N8ZGJtvXOmCzZfsXytkguzzT0gI1TdibF11cb9TStTsJIw8XYHSX5H_c-fm9G8AAAD__2tVdmg) in June 2025, sharpening international pressure for a diplomatic breakthrough. Movement finally came after President Trump entered the negotiations directly, a step that helped break a longstanding impasse. The ceasefire is holding, more or less, but the [promised stage two](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyckL2O3SAQRp8Gd1gw_i8obhS520RKii2tCYyviTAghqx18_SR8whbH-nTd8772_aGPvj4_IYnmbqnLdLFgWqlstWUuabiidMOCoYNFIxSaan05tCH19Y3zuxEQF1DRk_jBOMwzLo5jFZ237sFaQYH8_BrsNA77SZY5p7cMjTe3GtKK621GkG3_eJmQmV7dItd0IpekfM1lfZ38rEetIdX-qDS2nQ2wRy1ZhbdQ8AqYL2uqz053kzASlH-YQHr7XLDVIITsHouSIFlPiXjiyWTTdHJfCCTTLt84l-UmdCSzAGj9CxtSEwCVizy8dA_3r_HJLo1We9E9_XL3Y0u_kklN8W4fPkQPI-T6NXzRB_-X62fyfph4F8AAAD__5wVh8M)—withdrawals, security arrangements, and a blueprint for Gaza’s future governance—is still unresolved, leaving the region in a cautious interim phase. _Flying together with our sponsor_

**➤****A Coldplay concert kiss-cam** moment went viral in July after a jumbotron caught Astronomer executives Andy Byron and Kristin Cabot cuddling before awkwardly jumping apart once they realized they were on screen. ******➤ NASA astronauts** Suni Williams and Butch Wilmore returned to Earth in March after an unexpected nine-month stay aboard the International Space Station, far longer than their originally planned eight-day Boeing Starliner test flight. Problems with the Starliner spacecraft forced NASA to keep them in orbit and bring them home aboard a SpaceX capsule. The pair spent 286 days in space, circling Earth 4,576 times and traveling about 121 million miles. ([See Pennies](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyckL2O6yAUhJ8Gd1j82NgUFLmK3OU2Kba0iDnE7GKw4GysvP3K-whbjTQzGo2-j9t8syGG9PxvNzDo85zgqBEQocyY94q5BKjZCyb6WTChKOOU8dnZEN9z1zjjAQTIBgwf1CBU34-8Wc3w8HzUg3QS9Ggl9Hp07GGVVFowNbImmHONccY5Z0rwttNuBMuWzjq9aLuQjoELmEv7mUPCFXx85xeUdslbE82KuFciL0RMREzHcbRbTWdGxASJflcipi0neJ9qyxfg6eAK1IdkI31YXFaaPd0hpQCV1hwd9blQrga6hRhDTkRMttDLhd9xf16JnPISHJHXfycxOOodyt4U4_bj7Fc1kI49Nxvi70n8C9CXET8BAAD__4aihJg)) **➤ The decade-long** hunt for James Howells’ lost Bitcoin fortune ended in August after courts shut down his final legal bid to dig up a Welsh landfill. Howells, an IT engineer, lost access to 8,000 Bitcoin in 2013 after his then-girlfriend mistakenly threw away a hard drive containing the wallet’s private key during a cleanup, when the cryptocurrency was worth little and the key hadn’t been memorized or backed up. He later floated increasingly bold recovery plans, from AI-guided searches and robot dogs to buying the dump outright, but judges ruled the landfill belongs to the city and isn’t worth the environmental risk, leaving what’s now about $700 million permanently locked out of reach. clean_body: Today, we’re looking back at the biggest stories of 2025. | | | | | | Thursday, January 1, 2026 ---|--- Happy New Year! On this day in 45 B.C., January 1 was officially recognized as the under the Julian calendar, a system that continues to shape how much of the world still marks time today. In this special New Year’s edition, we’re looking back at 2025 through two lenses. First, we’ve ranked what we see as the five biggest stories of the year, the headlines that most shaped the national conversation. Then, we’re sharing five editors’ picks, stories that may not have topped every list but stood out to us for their impact, significance, or sheer interest. Of course, narrowing a full year down to just 10 stories wasn’t easy. There were plenty of moments that could have earned a spot, and that’s where we want to hear from you. What did we miss? What didn’t warrant inclusion? Tell us what defined 2025 in your view. If you’re ready to finally take control of your weight and your health, today's sponsor, , makes it simple to start—with trusted GLP-1 treatments prescribed online and delivered straight to your door. If you’re looking for a more expansive year-in-review, . In a special episode, Ayla Brown goes month by month, breaking down what we see as the top story from each month of 2025. If a story you think defined the year isn’t included in this edition, there’s a good chance Ayla covered it there. If your top story didn’t crack our top five here, it may have made our top 12, and Ayla runs through them all. However you slice it, this edition is about reflecting, debating, and closing out 2025 together. We’re eager to hear your thoughts. | Jan. 20: Trump Inauguration On January 20, 2025, Donald Trump was in a ceremony moved into the U.S. Capitol Rotunda because of cold weather. Carrie Underwood performed America the Beautiful to guests that included Elon Musk, Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg, Amazon’s Jeff Bezos, and TikTok CEO Shou Chew. The administration moved quickly to implement a number of major new programs, including a reciprocal tariff program that transformed global trade policy. The policy has been challenged, but it remains in effect while it’s Early on, President Trump also directed DHS and ICE to intensify immigration enforcement, including expanded interior raids in multiple cities, often heightening tensions with local officials and sparking numerous court appeals. Enforcement operations are . Also in his first week, Trump created the Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) to push spending reductions. Headed by Elon Musk, it became closely tied to efforts to dismantle some federal agencies, including USAID and its foreign aid program. Musk has now moved on, but the cost-cutting efforts continue. In a December speech marking his first year in office, Trump on the economy, immigration, tax reform, and healthcare. May 8: First American Pope Cardinal Robert Francis Prevost of Chicago was on May 8, 2025, becoming the first American pontiff in the Catholic Church's 2,000-year history after cardinals chose him on the second day of the conclave. The 69-year-old Augustinian missionary, who spent decades working in Peru where he became a naturalized citizen, was considered a Vatican insiders believed an American pope was unrealistic given U.S. geopolitical power, but his missionary background and centrist approach won over the 133 cardinals. Leo chose his papal name in honor of Pope Leo XIII, who developed modern Catholic social teaching during the Second Industrial Revolution, signaling his intent to address challenges of artificial intelligence and technology. . In late 2025, Leo completed , visiting Turkey and Lebanon to commemorate the 1,700th anniversary of the Council of Nicaea with Ecumenical Patriarch Bartholomew and pray at the site of the 2020 Beirut explosion. Sept. 10: Charlie Kirk Slain Christian conservative activist while speaking at an outdoor debate event for his American Comeback Tour at Utah Valley University on Sept. 10, 2025. Kirk was fielding questions before an audience of roughly 3,000 people when he was fatally shot by a sniper positioned on a nearby rooftop. A disturbing level of celebratory commentary on social media followed the attack, resulting in aggressive doxxing and subsequent . Despite the political fury, Kirk's widow, Erika Kirk, made headlines by publicly . The accused gunman, Tyler James Robinson, is facing aggravated murder charges and the death penalty, with his first taking place in December. Oct. 1: Government Shutdown The U.S. federal government endured its , lasting 43 days from Oct. 1 to Nov. 12, 2025. The political impasse centered on healthcare funding, as Senate Democrats refused to support the Republican-led funding measures without an extension of expanded Affordable Care Act subsidies. Approximately , while roughly 730,000 continued to work without pay, severely straining critical public services, including the national air travel system. Due to an increase in air traffic controller sick calls stemming from financial stress and fatigue, the Federal Aviation Administration (FAA) was forced to take the unprecedented step of ordering airlines at 40 major U.S. airports in early November, severely disrupting travel until the government finally reopened on November 12. The expansion of Affordable Care Act premium on Dec. 31, 2025, after Congress failed to pass either a Democratic extension or a Republican replacement, leaving approximately 22 million Americans facing sharply increased insurance costs in January. Oct. 9: Israel-Hamas Ceasefire The long-awaited was signed on October 9, 2025, and took effect the next day, finally pausing a conflict that had stretched nearly two years. The war began with Hamas’s October 7, 2023, attacks on southern Israel, when the terrorist group killed 1,200 people and took 251 hostages. The war continued through cycles of Israeli operations in Gaza, driven by stalled hostage negotiations and Hamas’s refusal to release remaining captives. Tensions spiked further after coordinated in June 2025, sharpening international pressure for a diplomatic breakthrough. Movement finally came after President Trump entered the negotiations directly, a step that helped break a longstanding impasse. The ceasefire is holding, more or less, but the —withdrawals, security arrangements, and a blueprint for Gaza’s future governance—is still unresolved, leaving the region in a cautious interim phase. DirectMeds makes it simple and affordable to begin: complete a quick online consultation, get approved within 24 hours, and receive your medication shipped discreetly to your door. | ➤**A Coldplay concert kiss-cam moment went viral in July after a jumbotron caught Astronomer executives Andy Byron and Kristin Cabot cuddling before awkwardly jumping apart once they realized they were on screen. Both were married to other people, and the clip ignited a flood of memes, including a fake Gwyneth Paltrow ad, along with intense online scrutiny. In recent interviews, Cabot said the viral moment led to public shaming and death threats. () ➤The Louvre heist stunned France in October after thieves used a vehicle-mounted lift and power tools to break into the Galerie d’Apollon and steal eight pieces of French Crown Jewels worth an estimated $102 million. French police later arrested what they believe to be the entire four-person crew—all from working-class northern Paris suburbs, with backgrounds including jobs like garbage collector and delivery driver rather than elite professionals. The stolen jewels remain missing. ()** **➤ NASA astronauts Suni Williams and Butch Wilmore returned to Earth in March after an unexpected nine-month stay aboard the International Space Station, far longer than their originally planned eight-day Boeing Starliner test flight. Problems with the Starliner spacecraft forced NASA to keep them in orbit and bring them home aboard a SpaceX capsule. The pair spent 286 days in space, circling Earth 4,576 times and traveling about 121 million miles. () ➤ The U.S. Mint ended more than 230 years of penny production in November after the cost to make a one-cent coin climbed to nearly four cents apiece. The decision was followed by a satirical funeral at the Lincoln Memorial and an auction of 232 commemorative three-coin penny sets that raised $16.76 million, putting an expensive exclamation point on the coin’s long goodbye. () ➤ The decade-long hunt for James Howells’ lost Bitcoin fortune ended in August after courts shut down his final legal bid to dig up a Welsh landfill. Howells, an IT engineer, lost access to 8,000 Bitcoin in 2013 after his then-girlfriend mistakenly threw away a hard drive containing the wallet’s private key during a cleanup, when the cryptocurrency was worth little and the key hadn’t been memorized or backed up. He later floated increasingly bold recovery plans, from AI-guided searches and robot dogs to buying the dump outright, but judges ruled the landfill belongs to the city and isn’t worth the environmental risk, leaving what’s now about $700 million permanently locked out of reach. ## Measles Outbreak Hits West Texas
A [measles outbreak](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyckDuy4yAQRVeDMlTQQh8CgkmUedIJVXwaizISLmCMvftX8hJe3H1u3XP_3babDjGc97_6QFXfPm0nthKxVsxbTc9SUw5YkgcG4wYMJso4ZXxzOsTPJjqnDHOSd6j4PM0wLVLwblfaoRBeLosd0AijjddeWiEHMCPzErqgrjDGGefAAFgP1jnL-bhYO4zzKIlg6EJNua87Vnzr4uMnvTD3Nh1dVHutz0KGPwRWAmtrrX-89XkdCayXBIH1ixFYjbYPGk6qqQl32vSH7qnRA3WJWGjG-j-f6GhN9Etcr5cvgbXLyj1biDGUaSaC3Q8d4rdB_dVcLwU_AQAA__8G83oO) that grew to 762 confirmed cases and affected nearly two dozen counties in West Texas was identified in late January. Two children died from measles-related complications before the Texas Department of State Health Services [declared](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyckLGOszAQhJ_GdEbeDWAoXPwNXf72SmTsdbDOxBHeC8nbn5xHuG6kGX0zmq_rcrUxxfvtv93J8Cvk5U5nScRMx8L5UTgfkUoOqLBfUOEgFUgFi7cxvZeu8WZVfoKGDOhB4zBOHTSbGWCC0cNAWmkXxuC1D2OPDv0awiV0TTQVpkABoEJULTrvHUA_OnfpdT-JTpGPnI-WN2J62RLSOz_paF3em2Q25kcRl38CZ4HzeZ6tW0sdX32Bc5UC551sSVRk_uH1IPstPyhZQXIjm3iTOYTook013hzGP86YUiyDFp267TamTyP_6Z6nwd8AAAD__-BddeY) the outbreak over in August. The first cases were confirmed in Gaines County, home to a [large Mennonite population](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyckL3u4yAQxJ8Gd1iw8WdBcY27_7VXWhtYxygYItgzydufnEe4ZrXSSDO_mT8_6w_64OPjNx5k-L2lNVItgZgpr5xehVP2VNIGCvoVFAxSaan06tCHz9o1ztyVm3VDRo_DCMM0d7rZTT_i0M8TolVzv1F3mzZlBztprSzaaWq8ucyUVlqDAlAtWOes1v1k7a0f-1l0ipznlFveiemNZQufdFJubTqaYHbmVxG3XwIWAUuttX1Wfl-igOWiFbBouP7r3AMe5GRE9ikWuftvMSsPwhKoyPSX75nwKSsVlt84eVCMKXqmInc8Se6YHUVyknfyWZ6eapEnWusjFQFLk417VR-CL8MoOvU40IcvLf_XtKeBfwEAAP__pnWJ9g) that has historically been averse to vaccination. The county reported 414 infections over the next several months. After the first fatality in late February, U.S. Health and Human Services Secretary Robert F. ## Proposed Muslim Community Investigated
A proposed Muslim-centric community in Collin County was the subject of [multiple investigations](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyckL2u3SAQhJ8Gd1iwxn8FRRp3N21KC8PaZyUMFmyO73n7yPcRUo5G-jTz_flavxxFSsdvd6Ll7z2vCe8akRnLyvmqnAthzTso6FdQMEilpdJrcBQ_q2mC3VSYdYNWj8MIwzQb3bys1_0cAMzgtzBukzdqHHbT98aEzXebb8g-MKWV1qAAVAs-BK91P3nf9WM_C6MwEOfS8gsZv13d4ye_sbQ-n020L-ariu6XgEXAct93mzYf9vupBSzPDQFLzN5FAcsPQBaXDixV5guTpPTGynQ4pvwkzhJdZXlFl7KkGt1JXnpMjEW6k4KsvvxlSh8BSzcpMyotYGmKDddNMVIdRmHUcTqKPxv5v4S-LfwLAAD__06Uhqo) and lawsuits last year, several of which are ongoing. EPIC City, which rebranded as "The Meadow" in November, was announced in 2024 but attracted official scrutiny last February, when Abbott [characterized](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyc0LHSmzAQBOCngU7M6QDJFCrSuHPalIzQnbESYTHS2dhvn7Ef4e939tvZP5f54mOK9_W339jJ65rnOx81sQiXWfJeJZfINV8RcJwR0CjQCvRMPqb3PLTkFqBJt-y0NRbNaRp0e3NETAHMgEFrsKZferKGDNkJyRga2-g-ZaBBawRE6DAQBa3HUwj9aMepGYApSi6d3Fj45es1vfOTSxfy1iZ3E9lr0_9q8Nzg-TiO7rP8YP73CTR4Xguvyi9LFlFrfHJVj528sNoeNcVNhShvtZf8l4OoL6AQRgA9tsXRfsSUYjW2GWDdfExfVn700dPh_wAAAP__tUJ2tQ) the 1,000-home community with a mosque and K-12 school as a "no go zone" that "is not allowed in Texas." In the following months, the project was investigated by Attorney General Ken Paxton, the Texas Rangers, the Texas State Securities Board, the Texas Funeral Service Commission, the Texas Workforce Commission, and the Department of Justice. While the DOJ [dropped](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyckL2OqzAQhZ_GdKDx8OvCxW3octstLcceklk5GOFRCG-_Yh9hu1McnfPp-7q5m-fE6-O_f5GVz5LdSkdJJEK7k7wVyTtTyQsC9g4Bhxp0DdpFz-l0XRXtHaLRFVk9DiMOk-l09bSTNkgEcWr1EBDaYMx9MkOcPPTG-HvF9hoDDVojIEKDIcagdT-F0PZjb1QHFFny3siThD6-LOnMb9qbkF9Vsk-Rraj2n8JZ4XwcR7PkT3fRXwWF8xUVzrRxqAPLWcf8XfP6piL88MJ5rUPKhWK127gdnBKXYVQdPF6e0--L_EnJ2-JPAAAA__-qN3Dn) its civil rights probe, Paxton [sued](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyckbGu2zAMRb9G3hRIlG0lg4cCgbfXtaOhSrSigrYMiU7ivy8cdOv2Vl7y4ID319f05RKlNf50Cw78nvO04qsSMmOZOG-Vc0lY8wwKugkU9FJpqfQUXKJjapsw_FbhphsctO0t9Ndbq5vHYJQyyl41-A7b2Rt7A-ugtZ29GoOtbtJwwpRWWoMCUBfwIXitu6v3prPdTbQKQ-JcLvxAxrerMx35ieXi89LQ8GDeqjA_BIwCxj97ZX7gKX_mAsZ47q4Lrixg9HkvXCW5l4DxA5OOOZcVDxlxxeJI1h2rxC156RMfMuATKW9YqjxJ0hFhxECHnIvbw04n2Yw7L1PNe_EozP0_i_6MFwxpX4S5z4jh38y7ZXMprsLc8c1YVkfyPJMuxoLRcS5VwNiUIWyvRJRqb0Wr4uISfR7A32rrOcDfAAAA__-UGKpv) the developers for securities fraud. ## School Choice Program Established
After years of failed efforts, Texas Republicans [approved legislation](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyckLHOpCAURp9Gu2sARceCYhu7f9stDcJFSdBr4K7OvP3GeYStv3wnJ-fPz_xjY4rH-tvuaPgdaD7wLgmZMc9MZ2HKEQsFJZSelVA9CAlCzt7G9Jm72ptF-FHWaOTQD6p_jZ2sNzO0ba_HVod-QS8W0WlEq0MfwmsR0g11NA9MSCGlEkqJRjnvnZT65VyrBz1WnUAfmXLDGzK-bQnpQxfmxtFeJ7Mxn6Vqf1VqqtR033cT6P3IP3ulppNS5OhKpabvG1a6wC4LMUOJ61FAwhJTinTARX_dhhnOTGu2OyR7g7PnGY8VKAS44wHFbUQJ3EbRIVh_kbOMpc7Gn_fDKf1QdWLdbUxfRf6vnpdR_wIAAP__rL2IjA) establishing a $1 billion school choice program that will provide funding for up to 100,000 students to attend private schools. In 2024, Gov. Greg Abbott [campaigned aggressively](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyc0LHOozAQBOCngQ60u9g4LlxcQ5drr0TGXoIlE3L2Xkje_pQ8wt9_MyPNn-t89Smn--2339nJaz3mO581swiXWY5HlaMkrsdKQHomoLED7ADn6FN-z6qNboFosWWHZjQ0XqzCdnOakHhQ2sa4LCsPlkY92FHDwkaZaNrkPmWAgEhABD2FGAOivoQwaKNto4BjkqP0srHwy9c1v48nlz4ce5vdJvKozfCroamh6TzPjwt8Fy717z9f-OMamr7RhiZfJIXMM8UFmePQrdZyp7SFzqMfO1DAmlYT0Kt-kz23xcXHmXJOdTSNgtvuU_6Oy4-eejr6HwAA__8M33Xb) for Texas House candidates who promised to support a school choice program, ousting several Republicans who opposed previous efforts. On May 3, [Abbott signed legislation](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyckL2OqzAQhZ8GOiN7-AuFi9vQ5bZbImMPMFrDIHtEkrdfJfsGW5-jo_N9X_fp7ijSsf53O1p5Ljwd-MgRRTBNwmcWToSZF9DQTqChU9oobabgKL6mpgx21mEwJVrTdz10t6Ex5Wa1C12tYcDaN1DPYakRlgGXGZ0b2nopyb7HtNHGgAbQFfgQvDHtzfu67duhaDQGEk6VbCj4dHmJL74wVZ73MtpN5MxF_a-AsYBx5av6lKqVrwLGN0UB48lZflNMByfl5plFVKb1yCq6I-wufavsN-ao_MbkUUVcKUcnxIeiQ1hF9yiTDeeDYqTc9UWj191R_ByRP1m7LPwEAAD__33Afzg) creating parent-directed education savings accounts. Despite more than $8 billion in new funding for Texas public schools, Democrats staunchly [opposed](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyckL2OKyEMhZ-G6RyB5yeTguI2dHvbW45Y8CxoSRyBNyRvfzV5hG1cWPan851_H9uHzyXfvv76K1l57rzdqLdCIlQ34XsTrpka76hx3lDjAtqANlv0uby2aYj2U8eLGcia83LGZb1MZkg27nFapnWP875ToGX1l3FcF6Np1hOZMGR7wLTRxqBG1CcMMQZj5jWEcT7PFzVpilm4niSR0NO3vbz4QfUU-DoUm0TuTY1_FDqFrvd-CiWHb0z804Rvx5VCd9godO9_he6wUOj0e4wK3b3mhxeCFhJzgQf_hES1ga8EjQSE4ZMCXwmK75Bv8CZBokoNEneQRC_ouRToXL8VuqHaeD8WuS1nNemvq8_lnVl-VfDD4v8AAAD____gi0I) the new program, claiming it would primarily benefit wealthy families, drain resources from public schools, and reduce accountability for student success. Students with disabilities are eligible for additional funding. If demand exceeds the initially allocated $1 billion, students from low-income families will receive priority. Applications for families interested in participating in the program will be accepted [starting](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyckL_O3CAQxJ8Gd5xgffhP4SKNuy9tSgsvy2ekPUDAne_y9JFTpU43mpFGM79fX9uXDRzi90_7oKW9fdoinZWpNSpbS7m2VALV5EGB2UDBIJWWSm_OBv5s984tu3Kz7mjR4zDCMM133R3L4N1ssCcYejOpwezeorXG42h3D_3UheUqU1ppDQpA3QCdQ63NhNib0czirsiFlsqtHdTobavnT3pRuWF6dLwcreUq-h8CVgHreZ63uKPz5xULWK8bAtacOLSAl-QUSdZmi_zHxPTIrSRmKtKHaDn8piorHimxxCMFJPlKTzyoyPJkqjLbQrFViTZKmzN_ZIjS016etnwErP1s1DQOAtauLC6fgTnUYRR39f2wgf-ub_-F-rXAnwAAAP__nLSREg)[ next month](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyckL_O3CAQxJ8Gd5xgffhP4SKNuy9tSgsvy2ekPUDAne_y9JFTpU43mpFGM79fX9uXDRzi90_7oKW9fdoinZWpNSpbS7m2VALV5EGB2UDBIJWWSm_OBv5s984tu3Kz7mjR4zDCMM133R3L4N1ssCcYejOpwezeorXG42h3D_3UheUqU1ppDQpA3QCdQ63NhNib0czirsiFlsqtHdTobavnT3pRuWF6dLwcreUq-h8CVgHreZ63uKPz5xULWK8bAtacOLSAl-QUSdZmi_zHxPTIrSRmKtKHaDn8piorHimxxCMFJPlKTzyoyPJkqjLbQrFViTZKmzN_ZIjS016etnwErP1s1DQOAtauLC6fgTnUYRR39f2wgf-ub_-F-rXAnwAAAP__nLSREg).
## Flash Floods Hit Hill Country, Central Texas
In the early morning hours of Friday, July 4, torrential rain pounded the Texas Hill Country, producing [flash floods](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyc0MFqwzAQBNCvkW82q41sxwcdSsG39FTo0aylVSJQrCAtcfL3xf2E3oc3zPxclgvFFLfrF93ZyivkZeO9Jhbhskh-VMklcs0BAfsFAYcWdAt68RTTezGNtyv4STds9TiMOJwno5ubJTdNxjCRDuB6NAOSA3P2zCu4sOom2gMDDVojIEKHznundX927tSP_aQMsI-SSyc3Fn5RDemdn1w6l-9NsjeRR1WnD4Wzwnnf924tUWjboqMjonDmJ2-icP7kTQql9vtQ2pBy9rXNoT02NcX6xx5TinUYlYHrnWL6q5B__fG0-BsAAP__1X1vhA) that killed 119 people in Kerr County, including 27 campers and counselors at Camp Mystic. The following day, flash floods claimed over a dozen more lives in and around Austin. Rain fell at a rate of up to four inches per hour in some areas, causing the Guadalupe River to [rise 26 feet](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyckM2upCAQRp8Gd5qiFG0WLCaZuOvZTCazNAiFTYLSgbr98_Y39iPc9ZecnO_8vy5XG1M8tj92J8OvkJeDnjURM5WF871yLpFqDgioFgQcW5AtyMXbmN7L0HizgteyISOnccLxogfZ3Iz2DpRbyeo1KB8mqa1f1eA0IoFTQxPNCQMJUiIgQofOeyelujjXq0lpMQD5yLl0fCOml60hvfODSufy3iRzY75X0f8SOAuc7epO8W7L5yxw_vdX4Bys41xq6_LBJa5fTL6lFxfaqf0g25By9vHYBM7n1bfo5-hF_1tir3qpoW-K8fdnTCnWcRIDbLuN6aPAP-r1MPgdAAD__32heho) in just 45 minutes. During a special session, lawmakers approved a [package of bills](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyckD2yozAQhE8DmShJIH4CBZuQedMNKRkNoC2hoTRjY27_Ch_h5f11dX__HtPDhRjS-tftYPmz4JTgpAjMkCfGgxhzAMJFS20mLXUrpBJSTd6FeE1N6e1T-kGVYFXXdrrth0aVm1XN0PW9qQ3Ui-qhNf7pjGlbXz-Nq2Epg73LpJJKaam1rPTs_ayU6ee5Np0ZikaCD4y54g0YPo6WeOEbcjXjXka7MR9U1H8KPRZ6PM-zmvGVecMXwf3hjhV6_JJivcGEWVBYE4nZ7YcgtwBfIsIaKDoOmERIIgMdmAgEo_j_ipdoxBIRPRV6LLP1xxliDNR2RSPX3YX4ncO_cve2-icAAP__HXaA_Q) that provides grants for the installation of flood sirens, establishes protocols for weather alerts, mandates the relocation of cabins in a floodplain, and requires camps to submit annual emergency plans to the state. ## Redistricting Passes After Quorum Break
On July 9, Gov. Greg Abbott [added congressional redistricting](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyckD2u3DAMhE8jd1pI9H_hIo27lzalIUu0TUQrGhLf-u3tA-8R0hAgCM7MN3--li9HkdL-2z1xkp-Nl4RXiSiCeRE-i3AmLLyBgXYBA502Vhu7BEfxvTRVmFYTRlvhZPuuh24YG1sdkxnWUIe6HhoEM65mGHoD69qM3QCtD2tF0y1mrLEWDIB5gA_BW9sO3tdt346qMRhIOD_kQMEfV7b45hfmh-dnFadD5Cyq_qVgVjBf1_X4W5zcRwXzDaFg_rwpmO_wCmbT32NUMO_80m5dWURzDpiLLid6clEXLIU4aU76oBi15-8k-a23yBwo7TpjoCKZvNybHF67FPR32ihROTDonU99ZuJMQni7V3kK50UxUul61Zj96Sh-KOS_Kn9N8C8AAP__XbWSGg) to the agenda for an upcoming special session, sparking a wave of similar efforts across the country as the GOP aims to retain control of the U.S. House during this year's midterm elections. The move followed a July 7 [letter](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyckEGu2yAURVeDZ1jwvo3tAYO2kQdV00qVqg4tDA8HiYAFL3Gy-yrpDv74Skf3nL_n5WxCDGn7aa6o6eHzkvCoEYmwLJT3SrkErNmDgH4BAYoLyYVcnAnxuXSN06twk2xQy0ENoMapk81Fr155AUp5OzojOjUata6jA49qkJOxTdAvmJBCShAAogXrnJWyH6396Id-Yp1AFyiXli5I-DDVx2e-Y2ltvjZRX4j2yj6-MJgZzBjRUsgpmmONeWtz2RjMx85tToSJGMy3PWbjKoN54AN_-fDTr-_8x9uVF-R_ks2pUqDbi2Qi_20s8q-mouPfctoK1vp_OIVKJdh2d74p2u1HiDFUNbBObFcT4vsifarnXcO_AAAA___Vv4ar) from the Department of Justice identifying four "coalition districts" deemed "unconstitutional racial gerrymanders." President Trump [later indicated](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyckLHSqyAUhJ8GO5zDUUQLitvY5ba3dBCOCXNRGCAxeft_zCP83RY7u9_uv9tyMz744_7X7KTre4vLQWcJVCvlpcZUasyeStwQUC4IOHAQHMTijA-fpW-cXsFNoiEt1KBwGKdeNA-txnETI5peSTIrGueM7IR066aUmGBtvL7CQIAQCIjQonXOCiFHazup5MR6IOdrzG19UKW3KVv4xBfl1sa9CfpRayqs-8NwZjif59mmGHz1Nl4GhvM1hOF8gTOcQTGcxaVqfu6Jb_5FvJCpPHn7_5l4JpfNyb9N3MbjnqkUHw8T-G4SB-hl34Fosnbp9CH4MijWw303PnyR6q_-e2n8CQAA___j9n4j) he wanted lawmakers to add five Republican-leaning congressional districts, potentially boosting the Texas GOP delegation from 25 to 30 seats. After the publication of a proposed map, over 50 Texas House Democrats left the state to deny Republicans a quorum, effectively preventing a vote. Speaker Dustin Burrows [issued warrants](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyckD2upDAQhE8DmZHdmL_AwSZkb9MNkcduQ0sGI7tnmLn9ijnCyyqoKn36_v0sP5YiHetfu6Phd0jLgVeJyIx54XQWTpmwpAASugUk9EIqIdXiLcXPomtvHtJPqkajhn6Afpy0qjfzkBY7bd3YetXa0A5BexhbGMegOz9hTeY-k0oqBRJANuC8d0p1o3NtN3RTpSV64pQb3pDxbUuIn_TC3Li019FszGep2j8VzBXM13U1Ib31TX8XKpjvWMH8nQqPe3LZchEhIorCllFkPJ-PSM4eRVApTxQ2ZywsLpuzPbiIjJ4KZ3JMxyoCrRvX2fjzohip9EOl5bpbil8m_pXAl4H_AQAA__8F9IOu) and fined absent members $500 per day, while Sen. John Cornyn [asked](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyckL2SgyAUhZ8GO5zLjSIUFNvYZdstHQRMmEFx4SYmb79jHmGbU535zs_PdbramOJ2-7ZrMPRa8rSFo6ZAFMpEea-USww1LwjYTwgoOQgOYvI2pvfUNd7M4LVoghGDHFAq3YnmbiSgnsWsJM7KeQjK931QwgqnZ6c61URzwkCAEAiI0KLz3gnRK-cu_dBr1kHwkXJp6R4ovGxd0js_Q2ldXptk7kR7ZZcvhiPD8TiOds8pUnT5NDAczyEMx7M4wxHUKQPDcZkj__C4D2t2xVLlv49cHit3uWzvjQN0epBaN8X4_YgpxSoH1sFttTF94ulfXz0N_gUAAP__zsp2bw) the FBI to apprehend the lawmakers. ## Luka Doncic Traded to Lakers
On February 2, [news broke](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyc0L_upCAUxfGnwQ4DV_xXUGxjN9tuaRCu480gGGB1fPuN-wi_-pucfHL-vOaXIU_h_dvsqMt3jXPAK3ssBdNc4pFLTIQ5riCgnUFAx4XkQs7OkL9nVTm9CDfKCrXsux66YVSy2vQIaukUOKsGaHtn1CgWq6yAdVksuK4i_YwJKaQEASBqsM5ZKdvB2qbt25EpgY5KTHXZsODX5NXf8cRU27hXXm-lHJk1vxhMDKbrumrMR3gigykshsH04G8G08xgIsdgUk3Xjs0gnhb_JouZ7-bERPaTeUnGUXhzF4Mly735YMrchLLFcHNnTspV0u64yHvKXc-UeO-G_H9P-dF5p4Z_AQAA__-z735R) that the Dallas Mavericks were trading Slovenian superstar Luka Doncic and two other players to the Los Angeles Lakers for Anthony Davis, Max Christie, and a 2029 first-round draft pick. The trade, which tanked the Mavericks' season and ultimately led to the firing of general manager Nico Harrison, has been characterized as one of the [most lopsided](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyckLGypCAQRb9GM6agH6ITEGxi9jbd0Gqh1S4RLGDH8e-3Zj_hxbfq1jnnz_f0jRw4rr_xIFvfS5oiXSVQrZSnms5SU2YqaQEJ3QQSjJBKSDV55HBPuvV2lv6pWrKqNz2Y4alVu1nthmHRBtDozriZtO4Nae0Grcj3Elq2nzOppFIgAeQDnPdOqW5w7qvru2ejJXmuKT_qRpXeWJZwpxflh0tHG-xW61mar18NjA2M13U9Cn-mBsY4YwNjxrhzXMWVcqmiZvRUxIxlpzpjCGLjj9wtwt8dhU_RsRNnwLuIA1-U2e1F4FIpi8guiQ1z5pKiWDhzXNts_XlxCFxM32i5HsjhP1r9UceXhX8BAAD__9LkhUY) in NBA history. None of the players involved [were informed](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyc0LGu4yAQheGnwR0RjAl2Copt6O62W1oTGNsoGCKYG9-8_Sr7CFsf6ejT_-dr-cKUU9l-40GOf9a6FDp7JmZqC9dn59oS9bqCgusCCqxUWiq9REz5vZghuruKNz2Q05OdwM43o4fdWbjPYVWk11WBwdkSgaF5UtNtxRHMkNznTGmlNSgAdYEQY9D6OocwXqfrTRhFMXFtF96J6Qf7mt_1Re0S6jFktzM_uxh_CfAC_HmeF-rP8hkF-HJHAf6DfwvwiwCfogBvRjvZeVQC_F5P2fm7lFQ2mb8fKGMtIQWJhfda3jLiK3XJDSPJgAdJrhvxTk3m2iWWjTJ1mfFBrcuIOWOXB76opfDoQ3PxeaacU7eTMGo7MOV_cP6vyi8HfwMAAP__Z9-NlA) of the trade, which [shocked](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyc0LGuozAQheGnMZ3ReAIGChfb0GXbLZGxh2CtsZE9G5K3X-XqPsHtj44-_X_uy92GGNLjtz3I8GvLS6KrRmKmsnA-K-cSqOYNAfsFAbUEJUEt3ob4XrrGmxX8pBoyatAD6nHqVLObbXOTVoBAvQZyK6LburHryOob6H5sgvmcgQKlEBChRee9U6ofnbv1Qz-JDsgHzqXlnZhetm7xnZ9UWpePJpqd-azi9kvgLHC-rqvd8queuXD9LATO33aBc1qtwDn--2ulz8kFJ23iPae39PYZquRiPcmVKsuaXbBRHuSDlYWs45BTbYrx5xViDFUPooPHYUP8gvCPqj0N_g8AAP__j2V-LQ) fans and commentators across the league. Doncic, a perennial All-Star and the face of the franchise since 2018, said it was "a hard moment for me" when he [learned of the trade](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyckD3O5CAQRE-DM0ZN-z8g2IRsNt3QwtAeo8HGgt7x-PYr7xG-pJKSSq_en-f0tCGG_fXbbqT5u6Rpp7NEYqY8cToKpxyopAUB2wkBOwlKgpq8DfGamsrrGfyoKtKq73rshrFR1ar7Bv1S1-MAg5pnGAZovZ-pVWPT09BhFfQ9BgqUQkCEBzrvnVLt4Fzd9u0oGiAfOOUHr8T0tWWJV_pQfri0VVGvzEcR9S-BRqDZryMVviuB5mYVaADvaASacqTMRaCJf99W-rS74Ipkivd1mck6DmmXnORmP5SDexc5p1KoyHNNkrP15OUaNmlPewk0Vdb-OEOMoXS9aOC12RD_g_GPLH40_gsAAP__agqAtw). Angry Mavericks fans erupted in chants of "Fire Nico!" at home games for months, even after the addition of 18-year-old former Duke star Cooper Flagg, whom the team [selected](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyckL3OnDAQAJ8GOqPdPX4LF2novrQpkX8WsM5gZK-Ou7ePLo-QeqTRaP78LD8mxHBuv83BWt5rWk6-S2QRzoukq0jKgUtaCahbCKhXgApw8SbEz9LWXlvwE9ascegH6sepxXrXgNDZHseJVnKwTiNaeGBHvrcTwzDUQX9lgIBIQAQNOe8dYjc69-iGbqpaYB8k5UZ2Fn6bssZPenFuXDrqqHeRq1SPXxXNFc33fTclfFFF82lNRbNL6eKs1mi2TRWO7IS9OpNCZT_qMC_OwT2L2tOt9lCUNeXJYk2Mag2iJJuzRCNclCR1WlNn7a87xBhKP1QtbIcJ8V-M_Ne5l6a_AQAA__9h-n5B) with last year's No. 1 draft pick. In November, team governor Patrick Dumont fired Harrison after he reportedly [admitted](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyc0LHOmzAUBeCnMZsj-2JMGBiysP1VO3VEN_blx42xkX0Tkrev6CN0O9KRPh2d31_zF4YY0vcP3Gjk95LnREeNxExl5rxXziVQzQso6GZQYKXSUunZY4if2TR-vCs_6IZG3dse7HUwullHC_3dEBrdWeu0W4ztroNp3dIaq1q8N2E8MaWV1qAA1AWc907r7upc2_XdIIwiHziXC6_E9Ma6xE9-Ubm4vDVxXJn3KtqbgEnAdBzHZavp7ARMlOSzCpjqngufId1RwLThi0pwjyoXTDIkGZ8PlD4nF5yM-KBS5R8qlT5yxSpdfhauwZN0Ob2oVOSQkzwCr3JHPiXpn1tOLGDCIm83_csN28-mjH4_Qoyh2l4Y9b1hiP9W839d_BrhbwAAAP__ir6NYQ) to a young fan that trading Doncic was a mistake.
## Texas Lottery Investigated
Investigations into two controversial Texas Lottery drawings led to the [dissolution](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyckD1uhDAQRk9jOtB4-C9cpKHbtCmRYw-LI8Mge7Ls3j5ij5D-0_v03tdtvtkQw37_tBsZeS4873TmSCKUZuEjC6dAmRcEbGcE7ErQJejZ2xBfc1N48w1-1AUZ3Xc9dsPY6GI1VtfgmhEWb4dW4zCSpa7GFsB5aqwvgrlgoEFrBESo0HnvtG4H5-q2b0fVAPkgnCpZSehp8xJf_KBUOd6KaFaRI6v6Q-GkcDrPs1r5Nwvvbk28BxfpGiqcjsQ_5CQrnC4JhdObVka-JF9ldnb3NpYSNophJ4VTkYw_zhBjyF2vGrhvNsT3r_wr0sPgXwAAAP__MPt3Mw) of the Texas Lottery Commission and a ban on online purchases of lottery tickets through courier services. On February 12, a Texas Senate committee [grilled Texas Lottery officials](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyckMFu2zAQRL-Guq1BbiQxPvBgFNAtPTboSVgvlzYbSnRJOor-vlA-odcZYAbvvb_NbxRTXG8_aRHXvkKeV9lqktakzC0_asslSs0BNQ4zahxBG9Bm9hTTPvedd1ftz6YTZ-xocXw996a7u55Hj8xs7GAHa65WB69R63Bli77XXXTHmDbaGNSI-oTsPRszvDK_DHY4q16Ljy2XU7tLky-qIe35U8qJ89Ild2_tUdXLReGkcNq27bTU9egUTrLCsyqcuMRFFE43WaVQUjgl2hb6kFKBKSUIuUBc_z5j2SGuLcN5WOD7DFI-HOzwh_jjkRscEW27FCDmZ5UKtVETyCFEjpQq5ACh0NMrnKjA5WL2X79_vHfF-ccWU4p1tKrXt4Vi-mZo_yX80-G_AAAA___BCZJu) about an April 2023 drawing for a $95 million jackpot won by Rook Texas, a Delaware-based shell company created by a [group of international gamblers](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyckLuOhDAMRb8mdBklhhAoUmyTbrbdEuXhGaINBBHPg79fMZ-wnSVbvueen-t0dSmn9f7tFjT0vpVpxVfNSIT7RGWrVPaEtdxAgJpAQM-F5EJO0aV8TF0TjRdxlA0aqXsN_TB2spmNboPTrUPvvYtRQOuHCKoPsnej9oNqkjmfCSmkBAEgLhBiDFKqIYRWaTWyTmBMVPYLzUj4dvWWj_LE_RLK0mQzE22VtV8MLAO7HlupdK4Y2JOVgRXdOY8M7KPysxYDm9aaInKakdcw44KcCvePg4NaOKXwi1S5WyN_pfVz9UnmuZxCDga22U3cXinnVHvNOnFfXMofJPqXv6eBvwAAAP__Af5-ig). The group purchased 25.8 million tickets for nearly every possible six-number combination to ensure their win. Days later, Houston-area resident Kristen Moriarty purchased a winning ticket for the lottery's $83.5 million jackpot through an app called Jackpocket. The drawing heightened scrutiny of third-party courier services, prompting investigations by the [Texas Rangers](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyckL2OqzAQhZ_GdKDxBENSuLgNXW67JXLsMRnJwZE9G8Lbr8i-wdbnT9_5us5Xx4nX5b97kJV3zPNKW00kQmWW_KySC1PNEQHNjIBDC7oFPQfHaZ_7JtgbhItuyOpxGHE4X3rd3G0wZEJ0CLGHEAkMOeODJz_eyNHZNWyPMtCgNQIidOhD8Fqbs_cnM5qL6oECSy6d3Eno7WpMe35R6Xx-NMneRZ5Vnf4pnBRO27Z1Mb9H912F18OicDpQFE6fcJvywbS3vL6oCi9OOK_tr1bculCpTbHhuXFKXIdR9bA8HKfPnPzpm5fFnwAAAP__Aq929g) and Attorney General [Ken Paxton](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyckb2uozAQRp_GdBPZY_5SUGxDd7fdEhl7IN44NrLnQnj7FdE-wS1HRzqjo-_P1_RlfPBx_W1eNPB7SVOkowRipjxx2gqn7KmkBSU2E0psQSqQanLGh3OqKzfM0t1VRYPq2g7b_l6r6jEg9p12ba-p0UuvZnKIUhvrrEHZz3Xlh0smlVQKJaK8oXXOKtX01uqma-6iluQ8p3zjBzG9TVnCmXbKN5teVRgezFsR-pfAUeB4HMftuX_TBQWOJrO3gQSOV47AMaSr6BQ4flTw_wYfdyrsV8M-RbApcr5-lBP-GvvcEoNN39lThkJ595YKrJlWMPOcmOFJETbz5hTBMKcc6YSVImUTwJmLcfb2KXDEvoO2s1I2SkPbaQc11TPccSHQs2mV1vPcdlTlwW2HD8GXthO1XF_Gh08y_2iffcB_AQAA___rp6Hx). The Texas Lottery moved to disallow courier services, and executive director Ryan Mindell [resigned](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyckLHOozAQhJ8GOqP1AgYKF9eky7VXImMvsDonjuxVCG__izzC34_mm2_-3ee748jP7a97kJXPmuYnHSWSCOVZ0qtIykwlrQjYzwhoFGgFeg6O4zl3dbALhEnXZPVgBjTj1Ol6t8H43o-OqG2HdoTF6GlBmoal034aA9ZsrzLQoDUCIjToQ_Ba96P3bT_0U9UBBZaUG9lJ6OPKGs_0ptz49Kij3UVepWr_VHir8HYcR3MtP4j-X4EKbzFdEqfyO9OqMhXenkW5VSirhbeNiijZaRW186V5qi9FIRjsO6yzDa-DY-RihqqD7eE4ftnyq6PeFn8CAAD__01ieO0) in April. Although a court blocked the implementation of the new rule, lawmakers passed a bill to ban couriers and [transfer administration](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyckD0O2zAMhU8jbzIk-i8eNHTxlq4dDVmiHQKyFYhsnNy-cIpeoBvJB3x4H3_d57unRMf20-_o5L3m-cCTE4pgmSU_WXIh5LyCgW4GA702Vhs7R0_pM7dVdIuJo63Q2aEfoL-Nra0eLjSL7RdYxxFjjLHxS7g1frC4gjENdBW5C2assRYMgKkhxBis7W4hNN3Qjao1GElyqeWBgm_Pa_rkF5Y65L1K7iHyZNX8UDApmM7zrH_z1f2KFUzXqGBakEWzeMFr-2IUTL4IhfQ9XV7a9Bq6f7lO-bL_6JD3nZgpH1qyXlBH4sUfEaP2_BeqN7-j3lBYH3jqgiyFglA-uCouPk9KibgfVGu23VP6dpf_evTLwZ8AAAD__0Irj4w) of the lottery to the Texas Department of Licensing and Regulation. Meanwhile, Moriarty was unable to claim her winnings due to the ongoing investigations, prompting her to sue the Texas Lottery Commission. She [agreed to settle](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyckLGOpDAQRL_GZB65GxtM4OASsr30QmTsZrHOjBHuWZa_PzHS_cBGFZT0VK_-fEwfPuX0_PztN3L8vZTpSWfNxEzHxGWvXI5EtSyo0EyosJMKpIIp-pSvSTfRzSoO0JCDvuuxs4OGZnW-XWzsWh-sRkDb2YABrJkNgOn6YJvkbpgCBYAKUT0wxBgAjA2hNb0ZhFYUE5fjwSsxffu65Kt80fEIZWuyW5n3KtpfAkeB43mej79red2lwNEfnEImgeOtI3DMJfgscHyD_qfM5fa8ZPZnfSWWlZgzbfRkufurvFjgiNbIhYy1UWtJ0BupY7vIWQ0gSQVFRL6fB2wOF_cz5Zxq1wutPjef8nsr_-jYL4f_AgAA__9aG4Xn) for $45.89 million two days before her trial was scheduled to begin.
## THC Debate Unresolved After Federal Ban
Lt. Gov. Dan Patrick vowed to ban consumable THC products ahead of last year's legislative session. Despite initial resistance from lawmakers, a bill advanced to Gov. Greg Abbott. The governor [vetoed](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyckD2OgzAQRk8DndHMAAYXLrZJl223RMYewFonjmAUJ7dfkSNs__08vZ_rdHUxxfv67W5s5bXk6c7lSCzC-yT5cUjeIx95IaB-IiCtABXgFFxM76mrg50hGKzZ4qAH0qPpsN4se_Qjk59Ru2ExY2i7URvt2lYbhtnU0Z5jgIBIQAQN-RA8Yj963_ZDb6oOOETJeyMbC7_csaR3fvLe-Hyrk91EHkfVflV0qehSSmlO8sL8ewYqunw6SjavZndXT5as1p1X5eY5iyiC0RDoerfhUWJK8dBD1cF6czF9LuRfPp6W_gIAAP__SN5uvw) the measure and called a special session to regulate the $8 billion industry. In his June 23 veto, Abbott [expressed skepticism](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyckL2uozAQRp_GdEbjwRhSuNiGLttuiYw9BksORng2JG9_lTzC7b8fnfPvPt9dymlf_7oHWX7FMu901UzMdM5cjsrlTFRLRMB-RkAjQUlQc3Apv2fdBLtAuKmGrBrMgGa8adVsdkE9uNjFBbwyY6c1mNDF3pvgAEfSTbKfMVCgFAIitOhD8Er1o_ddP_Q3oYFC4nK2vBHTy9WY3-VJZ-vLo8l2Yz6q6P4InARO13W1sbwG979y2j8RgdMHReD0LUvevFzcLp_ERa4nrdItS2GW9SCfXJaVak1lb04bjivlnKoZhIb14VL-fvKvBD0t_gQAAP__X4l2Ug) that the ban would survive judicial scrutiny, citing an Arkansas proposal that was [ultimately upheld](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyckb2O5CAQhJ_GznoE7T8IHFzibC-90GpDM4OWGSzosXff_uS9N7ispCp90qf687F-UEzxdf9NT57lK-T1xWdNLMJllbxXySVyzQEVDisqHEFpUHr1FNP32rd-3pS3uuVZT-OEo7G9bh8zDr0ZtVImaO-6wRjfobO9DUPogp1cG-cLprTSGhWiuqHz3mk9GOe6YRps0yv2UXK5yYOFv6iG9J0PLjeXn22aHyJ7bbpfDS4NLud53j6PN19lgwsViS5xg8ul0-Cy5xQluiv-sCDxPdZE8i7XKrDnQglo35lSBZffReC9P3LyFah80qtSBXk4SHTCPR9A25ZF4B-t8ouE4WDJDS44WnCowmTYwERooe8swbbZAIja9Wx4GHhry-z3M6YU6zg1vbo_KaYfPfmvL44Z_wYAAP__cKSY-A). Patrick continued to push for an outright ban. After legislators were unable to agree on a compromise, Abbott [issued an executive order](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyckL3u3CAQxJ8Gd1js-rugSOPu0qa0MCw2EmdOsLHv3j7ySXmBfzkzmtFP8-exPEyI4dh-mydpfvu0HHSVSMyUF06vwikHKsmjwm5Bhb1UIBUszoT4WdrK6VW5CSrSMPQD9uPUQrVrM_jRdxbaZkXsBxg6R9BiM7kRyI5dFfQ9pkABoEJUNVrnLEA3Wtt0QzeJVpELnHLNOzG9TfHxk07KtU3PKuqd-VVE80vgLHC-rqu2a7nh71zg_K0InG_rv5RbOqVZ18Qs6U32L4eTZMqOsuTdSuOZsjxoSxwMh3QU6U2I5ATOVdbudYUYQ-kH0artaUL8svCPjjs1_gsAAP__t4R-ug) to restrict the sale of consumable THC products to customers younger than 21 years old. While the issue appeared resolved, federal legislation to end last year's government shutdown included a nationwide ban on hemp-derived products with more than 0.4 milligrams of THC. The law won’t take effect until November, and state regulators have pushed ahead with [implementing](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyckD2OtDAMQE8TuqDEhL8ixdfQzdduiUJiINpAUOIZhtuvGGkvsI2bJz89--sxPowPfl_-mw01vec47njmgESYRopHppg85jiDgHoEAQ0Xkgs5OuPDNarC6Um4XhaoZdu00HS9ksWqu1naSUzVpCbTg6uVFS3UVppK9VY1XeH1LRNSSAkCQJRgnbNS1p21Vd3WPVMCnaeYSlqR8G3yHK74wlTauBVBr0RHZtU_BgOD4TzP8jsbuiGD4T6CwfBZYzDc8QwGKe_R_QK-xRdmblY0jp-eVp5weQZDfl_4itvB_e6emdLFTeYzOkwm8MnsPMS43d4iaXecPgSfm5YpsWzGh08f_emZLw0_AQAA__9ncoNB) Abbott's executive order. _Flying together with our sponsor_ **➤ Ozempic Isn’t the Only Option... On this day in 1863, Confederate forces under the command of Maj. Gen. John Bankhead Magruder on Galveston, which Union forces had captured in October 1862. Galveston remained under Confederate control for the duration of the war. Happy New Year! In today's edition, we look back at some of the biggest stories of 2025. Let us know which one you think was the most important in today's Poll. Yesterday, Arch Manning led the Texas Longhorns to a 41-27 over Michigan in the Citrus Bowl. Today, Texas Tech faces Oregon in a College Football Playoff quarterfinal in the Orange Bowl. Today’s sponsor, , protects your privacy by removing your personal data from the brokers scammers use—reducing spam calls, phishing, and identity theft. Abilene ☁️ 74/39° | Amarillo ☁️ 68/36° | Austin ☁️ 74/42° | Brownsville ☁️ 78/52° | Corpus Christi ☁️ 75/48° | DFW ☁️ 73/43° | El Paso 🌤️ 65/47° | Houston ☁️ 68/41° | Laredo ☁️ 79/47° | Lubbock ☁️ 71/30° | Midland ☁️ 72/36° | San Antonio ☁️ 74/38° | Tyler ☁️ 70/42° | Waco ☁️ 68/39° Take The Flyoverwith you. Busy day? Listen to our daily 15-minute podcast while commuting, cooking, or walking the dog—your Flyover fix, anywhere. | | Measles Outbreak Hits West Texas A that grew to 762 confirmed cases and affected nearly two dozen counties in West Texas was identified in late January. Two children died from measles-related complications before the Texas Department of State Health Services the outbreak over in August. The first cases were confirmed in Gaines County, home to a that has historically been averse to vaccination. The county reported 414 infections over the next several months. After the first fatality in late February, U.S. Health and Human Services Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. the outbreak as "not unusual" and vaccination is a personal choice as the CDC to provide testing and administer MMR vaccines. During the outbreak, there were and 511 confirmed cases in children under the age of 18. Cases were reported in more than three dozen counties throughout Texas, as well as in the neighboring states of New Mexico and Oklahoma. Proposed Muslim Community Investigated A proposed Muslim-centric community in Collin County was the subject of and lawsuits last year, several of which are ongoing. EPIC City, which rebranded as "The Meadow" in November, was announced in 2024 but attracted official scrutiny last February, when Abbott the 1,000-home community with a mosque and K-12 school as a "no go zone" that "is not allowed in Texas." In the following months, the project was investigated by Attorney General Ken Paxton, the Texas Rangers, the Texas State Securities Board, the Texas Funeral Service Commission, the Texas Workforce Commission, and the Department of Justice. While the DOJ its civil rights probe, Paxton the developers for securities fraud. Amid the investigations, Gov. Greg Abbott signed legislation that aims to in Texas and the Muslim Brotherhood and the Council on American-Islamic Relations as foreign terrorist organizations. School Choice Program Established After years of failed efforts, Texas Republicans establishing a $1 billion school choice program that will provide funding for up to 100,000 students to attend private schools. In 2024, Gov. Greg Abbott for Texas House candidates who promised to support a school choice program, ousting several Republicans who opposed previous efforts. On May 3, creating parent-directed education savings accounts. Despite more than $8 billion in new funding for Texas public schools, Democrats staunchly the new program, claiming it would primarily benefit wealthy families, drain resources from public schools, and reduce accountability for student success. Students with disabilities are eligible for additional funding. If demand exceeds the initially allocated $1 billion, students from low-income families will receive priority. Applications for families interested in participating in the program will be accepted . Flash Floods Hit Hill Country, Central Texas In the early morning hours of Friday, July 4, torrential rain pounded the Texas Hill Country, producing that killed 119 people in Kerr County, including 27 campers and counselors at Camp Mystic. The following day, flash floods claimed over a dozen more lives in and around Austin. Rain fell at a rate of up to four inches per hour in some areas, causing the Guadalupe River to in just 45 minutes. Over the following days, reports of , harrowing , and of the deceased and missing dominated headlines. Search and rescue operations continued for weeks. Celebrities, corporations, and relief organizations raised millions for victims of the disaster. Meanwhile, local officials were for failing to issue evacuation orders, poor coordination, and disaster preparedness. During a special session, lawmakers approved a that provides grants for the installation of flood sirens, establishes protocols for weather alerts, mandates the relocation of cabins in a floodplain, and requires camps to submit annual emergency plans to the state. In November, several Camp Mystic families against the camp, claiming gross negligence. Redistricting Passes After Quorum Break On July 9, Gov. Greg Abbott to the agenda for an upcoming special session, sparking a wave of similar efforts across the country as the GOP aims to retain control of the U.S. House during this year's midterm elections. The move followed a July 7 from the Department of Justice identifying four "coalition districts" deemed "unconstitutional racial gerrymanders." President Trump he wanted lawmakers to add five Republican-leaning congressional districts, potentially boosting the Texas GOP delegation from 25 to 30 seats. After the publication of a proposed map, over 50 Texas House Democrats left the state to deny Republicans a quorum, effectively preventing a vote. Speaker Dustin Burrows and fined absent members $500 per day, while Sen. John Cornyn the FBI to apprehend the lawmakers. The new map was signed into law on August 29 and immediately challenged in court. Last month, the U.S. Supreme Court that had blocked the map from taking effect. | Flying together with our sponsor Unknown number calling? It’s not random The BBC caught scam call center workers on hidden cameras as they laughed at the people they were tricking. One worker bragged about making $250k from victims. The disturbing truth? Scammers don’t pick phone numbers at random. . Once your data is out there, it’s not just calls. It’s phishing, impersonation, and identity theft. That’s why we recommend Incogni: . The trade, which tanked the Mavericks' season and ultimately led to the firing of general manager Nico Harrison, has been characterized as one of the in NBA history. None of the players involved of the trade, which fans and commentators across the league. Doncic, a perennial All-Star and the face of the franchise since 2018, said it was "a hard moment for me" when he . Angry Mavericks fans erupted in chants of "Fire Nico!" at home games for months, even after the addition of 18-year-old former Duke star Cooper Flagg, whom the team with last year's No. 1 draft pick. In November, team governor Patrick Dumont fired Harrison after he reportedly to a young fan that trading Doncic was a mistake. Texas Lottery Investigated Investigations into two controversial Texas Lottery drawings led to the of the Texas Lottery Commission and a ban on online purchases of lottery tickets through courier services. On February 12, a Texas Senate committee about an April 2023 drawing for a $95 million jackpot won by Rook Texas, a Delaware-based shell company created by a . The group purchased 25.8 million tickets for nearly every possible six-number combination to ensure their win. Days later, Houston-area resident Kristen Moriarty purchased a winning ticket for the lottery's $83.5 million jackpot through an app called Jackpocket. The drawing heightened scrutiny of third-party courier services, prompting investigations by the and Attorney General . The Texas Lottery moved to disallow courier services, and executive director Ryan Mindell in April. Although a court blocked the implementation of the new rule, lawmakers passed a bill to ban couriers and of the lottery to the Texas Department of Licensing and Regulation. Meanwhile, Moriarty was unable to claim her winnings due to the ongoing investigations, prompting her to sue the Texas Lottery Commission. She for $45.89 million two days before her trial was scheduled to begin. THC Debate Unresolved After Federal Ban Lt. Gov. Dan Patrick vowed to ban consumable THC products ahead of last year's legislative session. Despite initial resistance from lawmakers, a bill advanced to Gov. Greg Abbott. The governor the measure and called a special session to regulate the $8 billion industry. In his June 23 veto, Abbott that the ban would survive judicial scrutiny, citing an Arkansas proposal that was . Patrick continued to push for an outright ban. After legislators were unable to agree on a compromise, Abbott to restrict the sale of consumable THC products to customers younger than 21 years old. While the issue appeared resolved, federal legislation to end last year's government shutdown included a nationwide ban on hemp-derived products with more than 0.4 milligrams of THC. The law won’t take effect until November, and state regulators have pushed ahead with Abbott's executive order. Flying together with our sponsor ➤ Ozempic Isn’t the Only Option... | | Dorian Gray glanced at the picture, and suddenly an uncontrollable feeling of hatred for Basil Hallward came over him, as though it had been suggested to him by the image on the canvas, whispered into his ear by those grinning lips. The mad passions of a hunted animal stirred within him, and he loathed the man who was seated at the table, more than in his whole life he had ever loathed anything. He glanced wildly around. Something glimmered on the top of the painted chest that faced him. His eye fell on it. He knew what it was. It was a knife that he had brought up, some days before, to cut a piece of cord, and had forgotten to take away with him. He moved slowly towards it, passing Hallward as he did so. As soon as he got behind him, he seized it and turned round. Hallward stirred in his chair as if he was going to rise. He rushed at him and dug the knife into the great vein that is behind the ear, crushing the man’s head down on the table and stabbing again and again. There was a stifled groan and the horrible sound of some one choking with blood. Three times the outstretched arms shot up convulsively, waving grotesque, stiff-fingered hands in the air. He stabbed him twice more, but the man did not move. Something began to trickle on the floor. He waited for a moment, still pressing the head down. Then he threw the knife on the table, and listened. He could hear nothing, but the drip, drip on the threadbare carpet. He opened the door and went out on the landing. The house was absolutely quiet. No one was about. For a few seconds he stood bending over the balustrade and peering down into the black seething well of darkness. Then he took out the key and returned to the room, locking himself in as he did so. The thing was still seated in the chair, straining over the table with bowed head, and humped back, and long fantastic arms. Had it not been for the red jagged tear in the neck and the clotted black pool that was slowly widening on the table, one would have said that the man was simply asleep. How quickly it had all been done! He felt strangely calm, and walking over to the window, opened it and stepped out on the balcony. The wind had blown the fog away, and the sky was like a monstrous peacock’s tail, starred with myriads of golden eyes. He looked down and saw the policeman going his rounds and flashing the long beam of his lantern on the doors of the silent houses. The crimson spot of a prowling hansom gleamed at the corner and then vanished. A woman in a fluttering shawl was creeping slowly by the railings, staggering as she went. Now and then she stopped and peered back. Once, she began to sing in a hoarse voice. The policeman strolled over and said something to her. She stumbled away, laughing. A bitter blast swept across the square. The gas-lamps flickered and became blue, and the leafless trees shook their black iron branches to and fro. He shivered and went back, closing the window behind him.
---

Elon Musk just humiliated Donald Trump in front of millions — [live on X .](https://links.main.yourcapitalminds.com/u/click?_t=affa6bfca09a497f86454ba803e6a9a2&_m=3019b14b674f4724b78494c353a6fb61&_e=R8Yh1ESNJIV0pGpYuihhmm1Lj4w3moXiPDRvJ76xtpus8dIcDXfhhxim4XAlFI3NFyRrFkSIQ_02r9-2F_VH-mIaH5IV8OiBMb-nMMIopiP-CkS-1iCzMV6-oAmf1ofG0bo2lXrdTp1VeWPAAhAu2EWgU6myo6gla4zDdCVAhDqxnwwGWn3tQuVTrq7pLlb6F-AGkNr7JQL9IykYeFBfBBklv3bNKkxTyt10Gn347LBYS76dRiTsqnW-Pf_BmiEkqUjKn4JwYfVegjXK7iICtn-1dKuYKlmQdZNFv1OlQ7WJSF-I6UUmskNRsPbPjIlbCal5U-n4MaLN27SQSX9fFIH_4tIJnUWSQf8ahSs7Dyjx8M_rP1xd7DZcNozWLE0cckLw6FG3ISFZ-mffMASho8vwNW3U2XiPrOmPtwom9izzc-P68ZEzmBdt21ZnDUVelzPrKserIJ1MVTmLdq8M35F_CU3dA9b_4bmNYlyYun9obgTh2AcVKipipozdrDY0yW9RNCy8W5Xhh4_Oc_rPk8nJo8ecKZUi1RE4IfknMyR1htnpRV449_7Brsyyh54F_BshyqT2Or5x5AGklDOrLA%3D%3D)

What began as subtle jabs has exploded into a full-blown feud. Musk is firing off brutal posts.

Trump is lashing out. Their followers are tearing into each other.

And it’s all happening out in the open.

Insiders say this digital battle isn’t just ego — **Elon’s building something bigger**. A movement.

A challenge to Trump’s grip on the conservative base.

With over 170 million watching, this fight could **reshape the future of the GOP** — and America.
